mnemonics: COW stands for Copy-On-Write, a technique used in operating systems to avoid unnecessary memory copies when data is modified.
Imagine you're trying to edit a document. If you use COW, the original document is left untouched, and a new copy is made only when you first write to it.
keyPoints: Advantages of Copy-On-Write: Reduces memory usage, improves performance, and reduces the risk of data corruption.
Disadvantages of Copy-On-Write: Can lead to increased disk I/O, and may not be suitable for all applications (e.g., video editing).
When to use Copy-On-Write: Suitable for systems where data is mostly read, and updates are infrequent (e.g., web servers, database systems).
